description: Review the general features of muscle tissue.

cards.text: What is the S-V-P rule for identifying skeletal muscle on histology?

tip.tip: Three key features visible under the microscope

cards.text: What type of muscle has intercalated discs?
What is the classification system remembered as SSRA?

tip.tip: The five criteria for classifying muscles

cards.text: Name the triangular shoulder muscle named after the Greek letter delta.
What is the difference between origin and insertion of a muscle?
cards.answer: Origin is the fixed/proximal attachment; insertion is the moveable/distal attachment

cards.text: What type of muscle arrangement has fibres running parallel to the long axis?
In a pennate muscle, how are the fibres arranged?
cards.answer: At an angle to the tendon, like a feather

tip.tip: Uni-, bi-, and multipennate are subtypes

cards.text: What percentage of body weight do muscles make up in a healthy adult male?
What is the name for smooth, spindle-shaped muscle cells found in hollow organs?

tip.tip: Controls gut, blood vessels, bladder, uterus

cards.text: Name the muscle whose tendon is commonly harvested for grafts and is absent in about 15% of people.

tip.tip: A superficial flexor of the forearm
